description_long: This research institute is a large, multi-level building with a strikingly modern and geometric design. The exterior is dominated by gray concrete or stone panels, interspersed with large, grid-like blue-tinted glass windows that wrap around the structure. The building features two prominent, blocky wings on either side, connected by a central section with a recessed entryway and a series of black, angular elements that may serve as ramps or decorative features. The roofline is irregular, with various stepped and cantilevered sections, and the overall form is both imposing and functional, suggesting a focus on research and innovation. The use of dark and light contrasts, along with the repetitive window patterns, gives the building a Brutalist yet contemporary appearance. No visible signage or logos are present, and the main entrance appears to be centrally located, accessible via a broad, black ramp.
description: A large, modern research institute with gray concrete panels, blue-tinted grid windows, and bold, angular black ramps and features. The building has a geometric, Brutalist-inspired design with prominent wings and a central entryway.
description_view_0: This view highlights the left side and central section, showing the grid windows and the angular, blocky structure of the wings.
description_view_1: The right side is more visible here, emphasizing the stacked, rectangular forms and the extensive use of blue-tinted windows.
description_view_2: The front of the building is shown, featuring the main entryway with black ramps and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings.
description_view_3: This angle provides a clear look at the front and right side, showcasing the black ramps, stepped roofline, and the interplay of glass and concrete.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is centrally located and accessed via a wide, black angular ramp that leads up to the entrance. The entry is recessed into the building, framed by large concrete panels and flanked by glass windows. No visible doors or accessibility features such as handrails or steps are detailed, and the design is minimalist with no decorative elements.
